content.rendered:
“When it’s over, I sing I’ll see you in my dreams, but John sings when the lights are out they’re out, fool! And that’s the toughness in his vision that I’ve always admired.'”  –Bruce Springsteen, April 24, 2024

And who among us could ever see clear: the end is coming, it’s almost here.

That’s the most haunting line in John Mellencamp’s “Wasted Days,” and John generously gave it to his duet partner, Bruce Springsteen.

“Wasted Days” is a gorgeous acoustic meditation on how we live out our final act, and as Springsteen noted when inducting his friend into the American Music Honors, it’s 100% pure Mellencamp in both lyrics and music.

Just as the songs on Letter to You could only have been written by a 70-year-old Springsteen, “Wasted Days” couldn’t have come along any sooner. We’re fortunate for that, because it meant we received it as the first-ever studio collaboration between two of America’s most iconic singers.

And unlike their other collaborations on John’s Strictly a One-Eyed Jack album, this one made it to the stage. During Mellencamp’s performance at Stand Up for Heroes in 2024, Bruce made a surprise appearance (he wasn’t billed that year), and the two reprised their collaboration for the lucky attendees.

Wasted Days
Recorded: April 10, 2021
Released: Strictly a One-Eyed Jack (2022)
First performed: November 6, 2023 (New York City, NY)
Last performed: November 6, 2023 (New York City, NY)

© July 13, 2026

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Marc Anthony Thompson: Johnny 99
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Wondering how the founder of Chocolate Genius ended up in the Sessions Band? Listen to this cover and wonder no longer.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 21
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: This date in Springsteen history features guest-spots galore: John Eddie, Marshall Crenshaw, Elliott Murphy, Nils Lofgren, Joe Grushecky, Greg Kihn, and Jon Bon Jovi all share a stage with Bruce. Plus: rare performances, great shows, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Meeting Across the River: Bruce Springsteen and Phish, “Glory Days”
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Trey and Bruce tear it up at Bonnaroo and close with an epic "Glory Days."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 5
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: President Obama and an all-star line-up of musicians pay tribute to Bruce at the Kennedy Center Honors. Video and more highlights from this date in Springsteen history inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Bright Lights, Big City
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: At the opening show of The Bruce Springsteen Band's month-long residency at The Back Door in February 1972, Southside Johnny takes the spotlight when Bruce and the band cover Jimmy Reed's classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 21
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Born in the U.S.A. passes Thriller's run on the U.S. album chart, Bruce plays his first-ever show in Toronto, and more.
